name: Enchiladas Supremas
recipeIngredient: 1 Chili pepper, hot; seeded and minced
2 Corn ears; kernels cut off the cob OR 1 1/2 cups Corn, frozen and thawed
2 tbsp Chili powder, or to taste
1/2 cup Olives, black; pitted and chopped
recipeInstructions:
1. Drain and rinse the tofu. Put it in a plastic bag or container, close tightly, and freeze until solid.
2. Remove from freezer and let thaw at room temperature. Gently squeeze out water with hands, then between two kitchen towels. Cut into 1/4 inch cubes.
3. In a large bowl, stir tamari, peanut butter, and tomato paste until blended.
4. Heat olive oil in a large frying pan over medium heat. Add tofu, onions, bell pepper, chili pepper, and garlic. Cook until softened (about 6 minutes). Stir into tamari mixture. Add corn.
5. In a medium bowl, combine tomato sauce, tomato puree, chili powder, oregano, and cumin.
6. Preheat oven to 350°F. Lightly oil a 9- by 13-inch baking dish.
7. Spread about 1 cup of tomato sauce over the bottom of the dish.
8. Heat a dry frying pan or griddle over medium heat. Brush each tortilla lightly on both sides with oil. Add one at a time and beat for about 15 seconds on each side until warm and flexible.
9. Place half of tofu-vegetable mixture in center of each tortilla. Roll tightly seam side down, then pour remaining tomato sauce over enchiladas.
10. Cover with foil and bake until bubbling (30-35 minutes).
11. Scatter olives over enchiladas and serve hot.
